abstract: A selective listing by critics of The Times: New or noteworthy art, design and photography exhibitions at New York museums and art galleries this weekend. At many museums, children under 12 and members are admitted free. Addresses, unless otherwise noted, are in Manhattan. Most galleries are closed on Sundays and Mondays, but hours vary and should be checked by telephone. Gallery admission is free unless noted. * denotes a highly recommended show.    Museums    * ''A BEAUTIFUL AND GRACIOUS MANNER: THE ART OF PARMIGIANINO,'' Frick Collection, 1 East 70th Street, (212) 288-0700, through April 18. The short-lived Parmigianino (1503-1540) was as spectacular as almost anyone, ever, as a draftsman. A Mannerist prodigy of mercurial temperament, his best work is tender and fetishistically erotic. The show is not spectacular, exactly, although beautiful art is beautiful art. Including prints and a few paintings, the Frick's display is medium size, serious and just a little dry. But it picks up momentum. By the end Parmigianino invented his own obscure universe, a hot house art of long-necked women and frigid perfection. Hours: Tuesdays through Thursdays, and Saturdays, 10 a.m to 6 p.m.; Fridays, 10 a.m. to 9 p.m.; Sundays, 1 to 6 p.m. Admission: $12; students and 62+, $5 (Michael Kimmelman).
